module-3-club-check-in-project.label: Module Project: Club Check-In Tracker
module-3-club-check-in-project.summary: A neighborhood club needs one check-in desk that can count members, recognize VIP names, skip blank entries, and stop cleanly when the session closes.

try-module-3-club-check-in-sketch0.title: Open the check-in desk
try-module-3-club-check-in-sketch0.prompt: Use a `for` loop with `range(3, 0, -1)` to print `3`, `2`, and `1` on separate lines. After the loop, print `Open`.
try-module-3-club-check-in-sketch0.hint: The range needs a negative step so the values decrease.

help.concept: A range can count backward when its step is negative.
help.hint_1: The stop value is not included.
help.hint_2: Print Open after the loop ends.

try-module-3-club-check-in-sketch0.starterCode: # Print 3, 2, 1 with a for loop, then print Open.

mp-1-check-in-three-members.title: Check in three members
mp-1-check-in-three-members.prompt: Read exactly three member names. After each input, print `Checked in: <name>`. Use a `for` loop with `range(3)` so the same check-in logic handles every member.
mp-1-check-in-three-members.hint: Use one loop for the repeated check-in action.

help.concept: A for loop is a good fit when the number of repetitions is known before the loop begins.
help.hint_1: Call input() inside the range(3) loop.
help.hint_2: Run both the normal and edge-case inputs before moving to the next step.

mp-1-check-in-three-members.starterCode: # Read exactly three names and print one check-in line for each.

mp-2-count-vip-members.title: Count VIP members
mp-2-count-vip-members.prompt: Keep the three check-in lines from the previous step. Add `vip_count`, increase it when a name starts with `VIP-`, and print `VIP check-ins: <count>` after the loop.
mp-2-count-vip-members.hint: Keep the working loop and add one focused counter.

help.concept: A counter stores a summary that grows as the loop discovers matching values.
help.hint_2: Run both the normal and edge-case inputs before moving to the next step.

mp-2-count-vip-members.starterCode:
vip_count = 0
for _ in range(3):
    name = input()
    # Keep the check-in line, then count names that start with VIP-.

print(f"VIP check-ins: {vip_count}")
mp-2-count-vip-members.solutionCode:
vip_count = 0
for _ in range(3):
    name = input()
    print(f"Checked in: {name}")
    if name.startswith("VIP-"):
        vip_count += 1

print(f"VIP check-ins: {vip_count}")

mp-3-stop-on-done.title: Stop when the desk closes
mp-3-stop-on-done.prompt: Change the tracker so it can accept any number of names. Use `while True`, stop when the input is exactly `done`, and print the final total after the loop.
mp-3-stop-on-done.hint: Check the sentinel before counting the name.

help.concept: A sentinel value such as done gives a while loop a deliberate stopping point.
help.hint_1: Use break only for the done value.
help.hint_2: Run both the normal and edge-case inputs before moving to the next step.

mp-3-stop-on-done.starterCode:
check_in_count = 0
while True:
    name = input()
    # Stop when name is done. Otherwise count and print the check-in.

print(f"Total check-ins: {check_in_count}")
mp-3-stop-on-done.solutionCode:
check_in_count = 0
while True:
    name = input()
    if name == "done":
        break
    check_in_count += 1
    print(f"Checked in: {name}")

print(f"Total check-ins: {check_in_count}")

mp-4-finish-club-check-in.title: Finish the club check-in tracker
mp-4-finish-club-check-in.prompt: Finish the club check-in tracker. Read names until `done`. Skip an empty input with `continue`, print one line for every accepted name, track the total, track names beginning with `VIP-`, and print both counts after the loop.
mp-4-finish-club-check-in.hint: Order the special cases before the normal counting work.

help.concept: The final tracker combines a sentinel, a skipped special case, and two independent counters.
help.hint_1: Use continue for the empty string so it is not printed or counted.
help.hint_2: Run both the normal and edge-case inputs before moving to the next step.

mp-4-finish-club-check-in.starterCode:
total = 0
vip_count = 0
while True:
    name = input()
    # done ends the session; an empty name should be skipped.
    # Count every accepted name and names that start with VIP-.

print(f"Total check-ins: {total}")
print(f"VIP check-ins: {vip_count}")
mp-4-finish-club-check-in.solutionCode:
total = 0
vip_count = 0
while True:
    name = input()
    if name == "done":
        break
    if name == "":
        continue
    total += 1
    if name.startswith("VIP-"):
        vip_count += 1
    print(f"Checked in: {name}")

print(f"Total check-ins: {total}")
print(f"VIP check-ins: {vip_count}")

try_module_3_club_check_in_sketch0.title: Try it yourself
try_module_3_club_check_in_sketch0.prompt: Use a `for` loop with `range(3, 0, -1)` to print `3`, `2`, and `1` on separate lines. After the loop, print `Open`.

sketch-1.title: A loop-powered check-in desk
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown:
A neighborhood club currently writes each arriving name on paper and counts the page at the end. You will replace that repeated work with one Python tracker.

The first steps use a known number of arrivals, then the project changes to a `while` loop that stays open until the desk receives `done`. The final version skips blank entries, counts accepted names, recognizes the `VIP-` prefix, and prints one deterministic summary. Each step keeps the working behavior from the previous one.
